que: In SharedFlow, what is the primary goal?
options: skip state/data modeling
replace testing entirely
avoid understanding requirements
broadcast events to multiple collectors

que: Which API/concept is most central to SharedFlow?
options: legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
random utility class name
manual polling loop for all tasks
MutableSharedFlow

que: What is a recommended best practice for SharedFlow?
options: ignore edge/error states
use for one-off events/signals
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
optimize before verifying correctness

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing SharedFlow?
options: keep boundaries clear
follow official docs for API usage
write focused tests for behavior
using StateFlow for consumable events

que: For production code, which approach fits SharedFlow best?
options: Use SharedFlow only as theory with no implementation
Use SharedFlow while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use SharedFlow and suppress all warnings
Use SharedFlow with clear state flow and testable logic

que: A teammate asks where to start with SharedFlow. What is best advice?
options: skip running the app after changes
avoid feedback from tests/review
copy an unknown snippet without context
Read official docs (https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines.flow/-shared-flow/) and implement a small sample

que: Which statement about SharedFlow is most accurate?
options: SharedFlow should be applied identically in every layer
SharedFlow removes the need for any testing
SharedFlow is mainly about adding boilerplate
SharedFlow is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data

que: In a code review for SharedFlow, what would you flag?
options: Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
traceable data flow between layers
explicit modeling of UI/data states
small pure functions with clear names

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using SharedFlow correctly?
options: A small, working feature that demonstrates MutableSharedFlow in context
a slide deck with no runnable code
a large refactor without tests
an implementation with no error states

que: To learn SharedFlow deeply, what should you do next?
options: memorize definitions only
skip verification to save time
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using SharedFlow patterns
avoid official references
